summary: Joel highlights Mailinator, a clever service by Paul Tyma that provides instant disposable email addresses. The service requires no signup — you simply send email to any address @mailinator.com and then check it. Joel uses this as an example of great UI design through simplification, where removing steps (registration, passwords, configuration) creates a better user experience.
key_points: Mailinator provides disposable email addresses with zero setup or registration required
The service eliminates every traditional step of email account creation — no forms, no passwords, no verification
Great UI design is achieved by removing complexity rather than adding features
money_quotes: More proof that great UI design is done by taking away, not adding things.
key_insight: The best user experience design removes steps and complexity rather than adding features and options.
